feed.title: SAP Community - API Management
feed.subtitle: API Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Call rest service from SAP API management javascript policy
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

I am looking for an example where in API management a rest service is called in a javascript policy.
I've tried all number of things but nothing works.
Methods i've tried include axios, httpClient, fetch, xmlhttprequest.

Also not sure if the javascript can be extended with node libraries, probably not.

Thanks,

Tom

entry.title: How to test sap btp api without passing the cookies?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm trying to test my SAP RAP application in Postman which works well in the browser but cannot get the results in Postman.

I have done the authorization referring to this blog: Manually Testing SAP BTP ABAP Environment APIs with Postman using OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant


In Postman the status shows '200' still getting no results in the body: 


But it works when I copy the cookies from the browser and add them to the request header without even passing any credentials or authorization details.


Is there any way this could work without passing the cookies?

entry.title: Consume REST SCIM API of SAP IAS
entry.content:
Hello

I'd like to create a simple user management API to consume the SCIM API of SAP IAS. 

I got as a result : <html><body>No service was found.</body></html>

Here are the steps I followed :

1 - creation of a destination in CF :

2 - setting my xs-app.json :

 

{
  "authenticationMethod": "route",

  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/api(.*)$",
      "target": "/api/$1",
      "destination": "IAS_Identity_Directory",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    }
  ]
}

 

3 - updating the ui5-local.yaml :

 

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://sap.github.io/ui5-tooling/schema/ui5.yaml.json

specVersion: "3.1"
metadata:
  name: com.vesi.zfamanage
type: application
server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertError: false  # Ignore certificate errors for local development
        backend:
          - path: /api
            destination: IAS_Identity_Directory
    - name: fiori-tools-appreload
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        port: 35729
        path: webapp
        delay: 300
    - name: fiori-tools-preview
      afterMiddleware: fiori-tools-appreload
      configuration:
        component: com.vesi.zfamanage
        ui5Theme: sap_horizon

 

4 - requesting the right path with a simple ajax call : 

 

        onInit: function () {
            this._fetchUsers();
        },

        _fetchUsers: function () {
            var sUrl = "/api/scim/Users";
            $.ajax({
                url: sUrl,
                method: "GET",
                contentType: "application/scim+json",
                dataType: "json",
                success: function (data) {
                    console.log("Users retrieved:", data);
                    MessageToast.show("Users retrieved successfully!");
                },
                error: function (err) {
                    console.error("Error fetching users:", err);
                    MessageToast.show("Error fetching users");
                }
            });
        }

 

I tried differents paths and approach without any success maybe I'm missing something on the connectivity part of BTP.
From Postman, I'm able to request the API.

Thank you in advance for your help! 🙂

entry.title: Accessing token details
entry.content: Hi, We've got a client with a Shopify site and they require customers with a SSO on your platform. We require the following details of them but they aren't very tech savvy so can you help us, help them find these details, also any other details you think are needed on this please let me know:IDP Display Name: Authorize Endpoint:Token Endpoint: Client Id: Client Secret: Scopes: Send Client Credentials in Header: Yes or No?Grant Type: options below:- Authorization Code Grant- Password Grant- Implicit Flow- Authorization Code PKCE- Retrieve OAuth tokens

entry.title: Send processing log post processing of the XML/SOAP API
entry.content:
Hi,

We are using asynchronous API "SALESORDERBULKREQUEST_IN" which is based on SOAP/XML technology for creation of Sales order. Since this is asynchronous log generated post processing will not be sent back.

I would like to understand if there is anyway to send the log post processing the XML.

We cant use the synchronous API as the volume is high and SAP suggests to use asynchronous call if the volume is high.

Thanks,

Bharath

entry.title: Urgent Issue : Transport API Management with TMS to different tenant
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Solution for this Issue:Your BTP sub account should not be trial account.
If you attempt creating APIM instance with trial account, the attempt is rejected.

I'm trying to transport API Management to different tenant.
■What I did

  • I have configured these settings following below URL.
    https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/content-transport-using-cloud-transport-management?locale=en-US&q=transport
    =>Transporting Cloud Integration packages was successful.
  • I tried to subscribe API Portal Service and create Destination following below link.
    =>Couldn't success above attempt because newly subscription of API Portal is restricted.
    https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-api-management/sap-api-management/setting-up-api-portal-application-using-api-management-standalone-tile?locale=en-US&version=Cloud
    • Instead of above attempt, I created Destination of APIM which contains same config as the one of CloudIntegration except for the name.
    • And, added the property, "sourceSystemId.APIManagement" to the Destination of TMS

But now, I'm facing below trouble. Does anyone know how to resolve it?

  • failed to transport API Management

entry.title: Feedback on API Management Tutorial – Outdated Content
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I’ve been following the API Management tutorial "https://developers.sap.com/group.cp-apim-code-1.html" for SAP Integration Suite and noticed that some content seems outdated compared to the current version of the platform.

Could you please point me to an updated version of the tutorial or recommend a resource that reflects the latest changes in API Management within SAP Integration Suite?

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Best regards,

Sergi

entry.title: Error APIMGT100: Activation of API Management capability has failed
entry.content:
Integration suite can not activated API Management  

 

Any tips how can i solve it

entry.title: GST Data Exchange with API Integration in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Integration Flow:-

The S/4HANA to GST integration enables bidirectional data exchange with an API as an intermediary.

  1. S/4 HANA to API to GST Website:

    • Request Transmission: S/4HANA sends a data request to the GST website via the API.
    • Purpose: This request typically involves tax details or filings relevant to compliance.

  2. GST Website to API to S/4 HANA:

    • Data Retrieval: Once processed, the GST website sends back relevant information.
    • Display: The response data is displayed within the S/4HANA interface, ensuring real-time visibility and accuracy.

Now, I have some Questions:-

  • What are the prerequisites for integrating S/4HANA with the GST website using APIs?
  • What kind of API do I need to use for integrating S/4HANA with the GST website?
  • How can I configure the API in my S/4HANA system to communicate with the GST website?
  • What data can be exchanged between S/4HANA and the GST website through the API?
  • How do I handle authentication and security for the API connection to the GST website?
  • How can I test the API integration to ensure data accuracy between S/4HANA and the GST website?
  • What tools or methods can I use to monitor the API integration for errors or performance issues?
  • How do I update or maintain the API integration as GST regulations change?
  • What are the benefits of using API integration for GST compliance compared to manual processes?
  • How can I ensure that my S/4HANA system remains compliant with GST requirements after the integration?

Additional questions:-

  • Currently, we don't have any SAP Java systems. Do we need to build any Java systems for API integration? If yes, what kind of SAP Java system should we build?

I'm a beginner for SAP BASIS Consultant role.

Thanks & Regards,
Hemachandiran R

entry.title: 400 Bad HTTP Request
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community!

We’re encountering an HTTP 400 Bad Request error when attempting to send data to an external server via API from our SAP S/4HANA system. I’d like to share details about the issue and gather insights on possible solutions.

Issue Details:

  1. Setup: SAP S/4HANA system using API for data transfer to an external server.

  2. Error: HTTP 400 Bad Request received in the SAP system when trying to send the data.

  3. SMICM Logs: The SMICM logs reveal the following error message:

    ERROR => HttpParseResponseHeader: illegal status code: 610; status reason: Undefined

  4. Context: This error seems to point to an issue in parsing the response header. The status code 610 is unexpected, and "Undefined" status suggests there’s an invalid response format coming from the server.

Troubleshooting Steps Taken So Far:

  1. Checked API Payload and Headers: Verified the JSON structure and ensured that the Content-Type is set to application/json.
  2. Validated API Endpoint: Confirmed that the API endpoint URL and authentication parameters are accurate.
  3. Network Check: Verified network connectivity between SAP S/4HANA and the external server, confirming no firewall or connectivity blocks.
  4. icm/HTTP/max_request_size_KB parameter is currently set to its default value of 102400. We changed the parameter value dynamically in RZ11 to the maximum value, but the 400 Bad Request error persists

Questions for the Community:

  1. Header Parsing Issue: Has anyone else encountered the “illegal status code: 610” error in SMICM logs? If so, what was the root cause, and how did you resolve it?
  2. Additional Logging: Are there specific SAP transaction codes or logs where we could capture more detailed information about the HTTP request and response?
  3. SMICM Configurations: Could this issue be related to specific SMICM settings, or might there be configuration parameters to adjust to handle unexpected status codes?

Any advice or shared experiences would be greatly appreciated! If you've dealt with similar HTTP response parsing issues in SAP S/4HANA, especially around undefined status codes, your insights could be immensely helpful in resolving this challenge.

Thank you in advance!

Thanks & Regards,
Hemachandiran R
SAP Basis Administrator

entry.title: API Management is not working for me
entry.content:
When i am trying to create API Management, developer portal in SAP Trail account, i am unable to create it it giving error as 'Creation Failed' and when i try to delete the Failed API its giving 'Deletion Failed'.

Need Help...!

entry.title: How to call SAP ByDesign API from S4HANA Cloud System
entry.content:
I would like to call Sales Order API(ByD) from S4hana Sales Order BAdI logic.

Target is that when I save SO in S4 system, SO data from S4 will pass to ByD via API.

Is that possible to do? Is there any reference for this requirement?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: How to bypass a 502 Bad Gateway - ResponseWithBody error thrown by the API Gateway?
entry.content:
Hello all,

One of our API proxies is returning an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway - ResponseWithBody response. This is due to the API Gateway considering the response of the API provider incorrect, as it contains an "Content-Encoding" header along with an HTTP 204 response code, which is not a correct HTTP response according to the RFC 7231, section 6.3.5: 204 No Content.

Because the fix on API provider side will take several months to be deployed, we would like to temporarily bypass the error and "force" the response received by the API provider being sent back to the API consumer.

We have tried different solutions without success:

  • Removing the problematic HTTP header from the response using an AssignMessage policy in the response PreFflow of the target endpoint.
  • Forcing the response code to 204 in an AssignMessage policy called from the <FaultRules> section of both the proxy and target endpoints
  • By calling the API provider using a service callout instead of a target endpoint.

Unfortunately, none of these solutions works, and the HTTP 502 Bad Gateway - ResponseWithBody is still being returned by the API proxy.

Is it possible to bypass this error and force the reponse to a specific HTTP code ?

Thanks

entry.title: API Proxy Authentication
entry.content:
Hello,

I have created API Proxy with the API provider(On-Prem) and adding policies verifykey. Problem is that when i test API Proxy with the API key then it throws an error 401 Unathorized. Please see the below Screenshots.

entry.title: How to test API proxy
entry.content:
Hello,

How do I test API proxy in the test section? What will be my basic authentication username and password? Is this an on-prem username or what?

Note :- Configured API Provider With On-prem

entry.title: API Provider With Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello,

At the time of creating API Provider with Cloud Integration when Save and clicking on test connection then it is showing an error. How to resolve the below issue.

entry.title: API Proxy 401 Unauthorized
entry.content:
Hello,

After successfully creating API Proxy with API Provider (On-Prem) at the time of test API Proxy, it shows 401 Unauthorised. Please suggest how to resolve it, and I also want to know what my basic authentication username and password will be. It should be on-prem username & password or what?.

entry.title: SAP API Management - OAuth 2.0 authentication with SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

I need some help in understanding the SAML Assertion based OAuth authentication set up between SAP API Management (SAP APIM) and SuccessFactors (SF) system. I followed this blog post to set this up.

With an exception that I used "https://mySuccessFactorsTenant.com/oauth/token" end point to retrieve the OAuth token instead of using "https://mySuccessFactorsTenant.com/oauth/idp" end point, since this will be deprecated.

The idea is to not to expose the MyHr system directly to the open internet and to have security measures(OAuth 2.0 and TLS 1.2) in place.

The Proxy with this set up is working fine and we are able to connect to back-end SF system and do all operations. I have the following questions:

  1. How can I check the TLS version being used. I came across this post which says that TLS 1.2 is enforced by default. Is this also true for connection between the Consumer of the API Proxy and SAP APIM?
  2. Since the SAML assertion for OAuth with SF is being generated by a policy inside SAP APIM proxy here, can it be said that SAP APIM is idp here?
  3. New Requirements:
    1. SF wants to create dedicated Users for each Consumer to restrict access/authorizations - For now, I have implemented the logic to get the Username during runtime based on Application name and then use that Username to create Assertion/token so that authentication is done with User assigned for calling Consumer. Is this the correct way to achieve this? Any alternative way to do this?
    2. We are also required to use OAuth 2.0 for all requests coming to SAP APIM. I understand that one way to achieve this would be to have a service in SAP APIM which the consumer can call to get the OAuth token (Client credential based OAuth) as described here. This approach will result in 2 OAuth set-ups, one between Consumer <--> SAP APIM and another one between SAP APIM <--> SF.

Considering requirement from Point 3.1 and 3.2 above, what would be the recommended approach for the overall Integration i.e. to have OAuth 2.0 set up for end to end communication? Please advise.

Regards,

Faisal

entry.title: Default TLS 1.2 in SAP API Management - How to check?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

As per the below SAP post, the TLSv 1.2 has automatically been enforced in SAP API Management. So, my understanding is , any request from SAP API Management to backend system would be TLSv 1.2 secured protocol. But I would like to know, from consumer of API perspective, is it also the the same (request from consumer of the API to the SAP API Management) that the request is secured with TLSv 1.2 protocol?. Is there a way to check the TLS version in SAP API Management?. Much appreciate for any response!.

Limits in API Management 

Thanks,

Azeem

entry.title: How to perform POST using the Attachment API in Business Accelerator Hub Try Out feature
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to achieve this scenario: Maintenance Order already exists in the system and API Attachment is used to upload an attachment to S/4HANA Cloud, assign it to and create a DIR, then assign the DIR to the Maintenance Order.

We want to perform the POST operation using the Attachment API in Business Accelerator Hub Try Out feature since we were able to connect our Test system and was able to perform the GET operation.

Can you help us how to test that?

The POST function is under: Document Management > /AttachmentContentSet > Uploads attachment to a business object

See reference blog below that is technically it is possible but was on different method: Maintain Assignment of Document Info Record to Mai... - SAP Community

Thank you in advance.
